Note that some compilers (e.g. gcc 4.8) may use floating point registers for fast memory moves, so jemalloc must be compiled with such optimizations disabled (e.g. -mno-sse) in order for the workaround to be complete.

cfr.documentation: This is the API call to add a new entry in the linenoise history. It uses a fixed array of char pointers that are shifted (memmoved) when the history max length is reached in order to remove the older entry and make room for the new one, so it is not exactly suitable for huge histories, but will work well for a few hundred of entries. Using a circular buffer is smarter, but a bit more complex to handle.

signature: static zskiplistNode *zslCreateNode(zskiplist *zsl, int level, double score, sds ele)
reason: Allocates and returns a new skiplist node to the caller.

cfr.name: zslCreateNode
cfr.signature: static zskiplistNode *zslCreateNode(zskiplist *zsl, int level, double score, sds ele)
cfr.documentation: Create a skiplist node with the specified number of levels. The SDS string 'ele' is COPIED into an embedded sds within the node allocation. This creates a single allocation containing: node + level[] + embedded sds. The caller is responsible for freeing 'ele' if it's no longer needed.
cfr.id: redis|src/t_zset.c|zslCreateNode|static zskiplistNode *zslCreateNode(zskiplist *zsl, int level, double score, sds ele)

name: zslInsert
reason: Creates a new node via zslCreateNode and returns it to the caller.

cfr.name: zslInsert
cfr.documentation: Insert a new node in the skiplist. Assumes the element does not already exist (up to the caller to enforce that). The element 'ele' is COPIED into the new node, so the caller retains ownership and can free it.

cfr.name: zsetAdd
cfr.signature: int zsetAdd(robj *zobj, double score, sds ele, int in_flags, int *out_flags, double *newscore)
cfr.documentation: The command as a side effect of adding a new element may convert the sorted set internal encoding from listpack to hashtable+skiplist. Memory management of 'ele': The function does not take ownership of the 'ele' SDS string, but copies it if needed.

name: ziplistMerge
reason: Produces and returns a reallocated merged ziplist transferred to caller, though it also frees one input.

cfr.name: ziplistMerge
cfr.documentation: they are changed and free'd in-place. The result ziplist is the contents of 'first' followed by 'second'. On failure: returns NULL if the merge is impossible. On success: returns the merged ziplist (which is expanded version of either 'first' or 'second', also frees the other unused input ziplist, and sets the input ziplist argument equal to newly reallocated ziplist return value.

name: ziplistDelete
reason: Deletes a single entry, releasing its memory and shrinking the ziplist.

cfr.name: ziplistDelete
cfr.documentation: Delete a single entry from the ziplist, pointed to by *p. Also update *p in place, to be able to iterate over the ziplist, while deleting entries.

name: zlibc_free
reason: Wraps libc free() to release caller-owned memory.

cfr.name: zlibc_free
cfr.documentation: This function provide us access to the original libc free(). This is useful for instance to free results obtained by backtrace_symbols(). We need to define this function before including zmalloc.h that may shadow the free implementation if we use jemalloc or another non standard allocator.
